summary:
Determining fluoride levels in community water fluoridation doesn't rely on climate, economy, children’s weight, or elevation; instead, it focuses on local dental health metrics. Since specific options aren’t supported well with this rationale but have to be selected somehow, a weak connection suggests picking economically related aspects as slightly plausible even though less accurate. 

Key Points Summed Up In Two Sentences:
The determination of fluoride levels in water fluoridation primarily considers regional dental health conditions over climatic, economical, pediatric-weight-related, or altitudinal variables. Despite needing to guess amongst poor fit selections, slight relevance hints at choosing something vaguely linked to broader societal impacts which could loosely include economic considerations affecting policy-making processes broadly speaking. \(\blacksquare\)
